mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in RSAFS signals strengthening household demand that raises near-term revenue expectations for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2016-07-15 latest RSAFS snapshot was 456978.0
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by nonpositive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in FEDFUNDS signals tighter discount-rate conditions and reduced equity risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the rate increase is visible.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month maximum or rises at least 0.25pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2016-07-15 latest FEDFUNDS snapshot was 0.38
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS tightening episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or mean triggered return exceeds baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is already rising, investors price a higher probability of additional tightening, reducing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation crosses above its 12-month rate while FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2016-07-15 latest CPIAUCSL snapshot was 239.927
failure_condition: Reject if this joint inflation-and-rate trigger has nonnegative mean 60-day S&P 500 returns or interaction term t-statistic below 1.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth with a stable or falling UNRATE signals resilient income formation and lower recession risk, creating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change exceeds 150k while UNRATE is flat or lower over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2016-07-15 latest UNRATE snapshot was 4.9
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-strength observations are followed by nonpositive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or hit rate below 50% across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and falling expected earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2016-07-15 latest UNRATE snapshot was 4.9
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE deterioration triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or no negative return differential versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in INDPRO signals weakening goods-sector output and margin pressure, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its trailing 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2016-07-15 latest INDPRO snapshot was 104.1342
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-crossing events deliver positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or the signal coefficient is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: An upside crossing in HOUST signals improving construction demand and durable-goods activity, supporting earnings breadth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2016-07-15 latest HOUST snapshot was 1164.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside-crossing events have mean 60-day S&P 500 returns below zero or fail to outperform non-trigger periods by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in GDPC1 growth signals broad demand deceleration and weaker aggregate earnings power, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2016-07-15 latest GDPC1 snapshot was 16514.6
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 low-growth triggers are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or the triggered downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.
